```{r tables}
f_sens_uw <- make_rhs_formula("phenoage_accel_unweighted", c("mixture_qscore", "ckd"), sens_covars_rhs)
f_sens_both <- make_rhs_formula("phenoage_accel", c("mixture_qscore", "ckd"), sens_covars_rhs)
fit_sens_uw_obj <- fit_svy_with_covar_guard(qdat, "phenoage_accel_unweighted", c("mixture_qscore", "ckd"), realized_covars_int, "Unweighted residualized PhenoAgeAccel (sensitivity)")
fit_sens_both_markers_obj <- fit_svy_with_covar_guard(qdat %>% filter(!is.na(egfr), !is.na(acr)), "phenoage_accel", c("mixture_qscore", "ckd"), realized_covars_int, "Restricted to participants with both eGFR and ACR")
fit_sens_no_acute_obj <- fit_svy_with_covar_guard(qdat %>% filter(is.na(crp_acute) | crp_acute == 0L), "phenoage_accel", c("mixture_qscore", "ckd"), realized_covars_int, "Exclude participants with CRP > 1 mg/dL")
fit_sens_strict_obj <- fit_svy_with_covar_guard(qdat_strict, "phenoage_accel", c("mixture_qscore", "ckd"), realized_covars_int, "Strict complete-case (qgcomp-compatible PFAS subset), same 6-variable adjustment")

# Sensitivity: additional adjustment for cardiometabolic conditions potentially on the causal pathway.
covars_cardiometabolic <- c(
  realized_covars_int,
  if (valid_num("diabetes", qdat)) "diabetes",
  if (valid_num("hypertension", qdat)) "hypertension",
  if (valid_num("cvd", qdat)) "cvd"
)
fit_sens_cardiometabolic_obj <- fit_svy_with_covar_guard(qdat, "phenoage_accel", c("mixture_qscore", "ckd"), covars_cardiometabolic, "Additional adjustment for diabetes, hypertension, CVD (potentially on causal pathway)")
fit_sens_cardiometabolic <- fit_sens_cardiometabolic_obj$fit

tab4 <- bind_rows(
  get_mix_row(fit_sens1, "CKD redefined as eGFR<60", fit_sens1_obj$n_model),
  get_mix_row(fit_sens2, "PhenoAge excluding creatinine only", fit_sens2_obj$n_model),
  get_mix_row(fit_sens3, "PhenoAge excluding creatinine and albumin", fit_sens3_obj$n_model),
  get_mix_row(
    fit_sens_uw_obj$fit,
    "Unweighted residualized PhenoAgeAccel (sensitivity)",
    fit_sens_uw_obj$n_model
  ),
  get_mix_row(
    fit_sens_both_markers_obj$fit,
    "Restricted to participants with both eGFR and ACR",
    fit_sens_both_markers_obj$n_model
  ),
  get_mix_row(
    fit_sens_no_acute_obj$fit,
    "Exclude participants with CRP > 1 mg/dL",
    fit_sens_no_acute_obj$n_model
  ),
  get_mix_row(
    fit_sens_cardiometabolic,
    "Additional adjustment for diabetes, hypertension, CVD (potentially on causal pathway)",
    fit_sens_cardiometabolic_obj$n_model
  ),
  get_mix_row(
    fit_sens_strict_obj$fit,
    "Strict complete-case (qgcomp-compatible PFAS subset), same 6-variable adjustment",
    fit_sens_strict_obj$n_model
  )
) %>%
  transmute(
    sensitivity,
    beta = fmt(estimate),
    conf.low = fmt(conf.low),
    conf.high = fmt(conf.high),
    p.value = fmt_p(p.value),
    N,
    model = "Sensitivity"
  )
kable(tab4, caption = "Table 4. Sensitivity analyses (primary adjustment: age, sex, race, PIR, BMI, smoking, cycle)")
cat("\n*Table 4 note:* Primary and most sensitivity models use the lean adjustment set (age, sex, race/ethnicity, PIR, BMI, smoking, cycle). ",
    "One row adds diabetes, hypertension, and CVD with explicit labeling that these may be on the causal pathway. ",
    "Each row reports the `mixture_qscore` coefficient from a survey-weighted model (`svyglm`) with NHANES design variables.\n")

sens_diagnostics <- bind_rows(
  tibble(sensitivity = "CKD redefined as eGFR<60", N_model = fit_sens1_obj$n_model, dropped_covariates = ifelse(fit_sens1_obj$dropped == "", "None", fit_sens1_obj$dropped), fit_error = ifelse(is.null(fit_sens1_obj$error) || fit_sens1_obj$error == "", "None", fit_sens1_obj$error)),
  tibble(sensitivity = "PhenoAge excluding creatinine only", N_model = fit_sens2_obj$n_model, dropped_covariates = ifelse(fit_sens2_obj$dropped == "", "None", fit_sens2_obj$dropped), fit_error = ifelse(is.null(fit_sens2_obj$error) || fit_sens2_obj$error == "", "None", fit_sens2_obj$error)),
  tibble(sensitivity = "PhenoAge excluding creatinine and albumin", N_model = fit_sens3_obj$n_model, dropped_covariates = ifelse(fit_sens3_obj$dropped == "", "None", fit_sens3_obj$dropped), fit_error = ifelse(is.null(fit_sens3_obj$error) || fit_sens3_obj$error == "", "None", fit_sens3_obj$error)),
  tibble(sensitivity = "Unweighted residualized PhenoAgeAccel (sensitivity)", N_model = fit_sens_uw_obj$n_model, dropped_covariates = ifelse(fit_sens_uw_obj$dropped == "", "None", fit_sens_uw_obj$dropped), fit_error = ifelse(is.null(fit_sens_uw_obj$error) || fit_sens_uw_obj$error == "", "None", fit_sens_uw_obj$error)),
  tibble(sensitivity = "Restricted to participants with both eGFR and ACR", N_model = fit_sens_both_markers_obj$n_model, dropped_covariates = ifelse(fit_sens_both_markers_obj$dropped == "", "None", fit_sens_both_markers_obj$dropped), fit_error = ifelse(is.null(fit_sens_both_markers_obj$error) || fit_sens_both_markers_obj$error == "", "None", fit_sens_both_markers_obj$error)),
  tibble(sensitivity = "Exclude participants with CRP > 1 mg/dL", N_model = fit_sens_no_acute_obj$n_model, dropped_covariates = ifelse(fit_sens_no_acute_obj$dropped == "", "None", fit_sens_no_acute_obj$dropped), fit_error = ifelse(is.null(fit_sens_no_acute_obj$error) || fit_sens_no_acute_obj$error == "", "None", fit_sens_no_acute_obj$error)),
  tibble(sensitivity = "Additional adjustment for diabetes, hypertension, CVD (potentially on causal pathway)", N_model = fit_sens_cardiometabolic_obj$n_model, dropped_covariates = ifelse(fit_sens_cardiometabolic_obj$dropped == "", "None", fit_sens_cardiometabolic_obj$dropped), fit_error = ifelse(is.null(fit_sens_cardiometabolic_obj$error) || fit_sens_cardiometabolic_obj$error == "", "None", fit_sens_cardiometabolic_obj$error)),
  tibble(sensitivity = "Strict complete-case (qgcomp-compatible PFAS subset), same 6-variable adjustment", N_model = fit_sens_strict_obj$n_model, dropped_covariates = ifelse(fit_sens_strict_obj$dropped == "", "None", fit_sens_strict_obj$dropped), fit_error = ifelse(is.null(fit_sens_strict_obj$error) || fit_sens_strict_obj$error == "", "None", fit_sens_strict_obj$error))
)
kable(sens_diagnostics, caption = "Sensitivity model diagnostics (dropped covariates and fit errors).")

# Supplementary: single-pollutant models with same 6-variable adjustment as Model B.
single_pollutant_out <- lapply(exposure_vars, function(v) {
  sp_obj <- fit_svy_with_covar_guard(qdat, "phenoage_accel", c(v, "ckd"), realized_covars_int, paste0("Single pollutant: ", v))
  fit_sp <- sp_obj$fit
  if (is.null(fit_sp)) {
    return(list(
      row = tibble(exposure = v, beta = NA_real_, conf.low = NA_real_, conf.high = NA_real_, p.value = NA_real_, N = sp_obj$n_model),
      diag = tibble(exposure = v, N_model = sp_obj$n_model, dropped_covariates = ifelse(sp_obj$dropped == "", "None", sp_obj$dropped), fit_error = ifelse(is.null(sp_obj$error) || sp_obj$error == "", "Model failed", sp_obj$error))
    ))
  }
  tb <- broom::tidy(fit_sp, conf.int = TRUE) %>% filter(term == v)
  if (nrow(tb) == 0) {
    return(list(
      row = tibble(exposure = v, beta = NA_real_, conf.low = NA_real_, conf.high = NA_real_, p.value = NA_real_, N = sp_obj$n_model),
      diag = tibble(exposure = v, N_model = sp_obj$n_model, dropped_covariates = ifelse(sp_obj$dropped == "", "None", sp_obj$dropped), fit_error = "Exposure term absent")
    ))
  }
  list(
    row = tibble(
      exposure = v,
      beta = tb$estimate[1],
      conf.low = if ("conf.low" %in% names(tb)) tb$conf.low[1] else NA_real_,
      conf.high = if ("conf.high" %in% names(tb)) tb$conf.high[1] else NA_real_,
      p.value = if ("p.value" %in% names(tb)) tb$p.value[1] else NA_real_,
      N = sp_obj$n_model
    ),
    diag = tibble(exposure = v, N_model = sp_obj$n_model, dropped_covariates = ifelse(sp_obj$dropped == "", "None", sp_obj$dropped), fit_error = ifelse(is.null(sp_obj$error) || sp_obj$error == "", "None", sp_obj$error))
  )
})
single_pollutant <- bind_rows(lapply(single_pollutant_out, `[[`, "row")) %>%
  mutate(
    beta = fmt(beta),
    conf.low = fmt(conf.low),
    conf.high = fmt(conf.high),
    p.value = fmt_p(p.value)
  )
single_pollutant_diagnostics <- bind_rows(lapply(single_pollutant_out, `[[`, "diag"))

kable(
  single_pollutant,
  caption = "Supplementary Table. Single-pollutant associations with PhenoAge acceleration (survey-weighted, same 6-variable adjustment as Model B)."
)
kable(single_pollutant_diagnostics, caption = "Supplementary model diagnostics (single-pollutant dropped covariates and fit errors).")

# Middle-aged men subgroup tables (age window defined in config).
middle_men <- qdat %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(age),
    age >= config$middle_age_min,
    age <= config$middle_age_max,
    !is.na(sex),
    toupper(as.character(sex)) == "MALE"
  )

if (nrow(middle_men) > 0) {
  middle_men <- repool_weights(middle_men)
  covars_mam <- c(
    if (valid_cat("sex", middle_men)) "factor(sex)",
    if (valid_cat("race_eth", middle_men)) "factor(race_eth)",
    if (valid_num("pir", middle_men)) "pir",
    if (valid_num("smoker", middle_men)) "smoker",
    if (valid_num("bmi", middle_men)) "bmi",
    if (valid_cat("cycle", middle_men)) "factor(cycle)"
  )

  # MAM Table 2 (descriptive overall qgcomp)
  qg_exposure_vars_mam <- qg_exposure_vars[vapply(qg_exposure_vars, function(v) {
    x <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(middle_men[[v]]))
    x <- x[is.finite(x)]
    length(x) > 0 && dplyr::n_distinct(x) >= 2
  }, logical(1))]
  covars_qg_mam <- covars_mam
  build_qg_mam_formula <- function(exps, covs) {
    rhs <- paste(c(exps, covs), collapse = " + ")
    as.formula(paste("phenoage_accel ~", rhs))
  }
  f_qg_mam <- build_qg_mam_formula(qg_exposure_vars_mam, covars_qg_mam)
  mam_qg <- middle_men
  repeat {
    mf_qg_mam <- tryCatch(model.frame(f_qg_mam, data = mam_qg, na.action = na.omit), error = function(e) NULL)
    if (is.null(mf_qg_mam) || nrow(mf_qg_mam) == 0) break
    factor_terms_mam <- names(mf_qg_mam)[vapply(mf_qg_mam, is.factor, logical(1))]
    bad_factor_terms_mam <- factor_terms_mam[vapply(mf_qg_mam[factor_terms_mam], function(x) nlevels(droplevels(x)) < 2, logical(1))]
    removable_mam <- intersect(covars_qg_mam, bad_factor_terms_mam)
    if (length(removable_mam) == 0) break
    covars_qg_mam <- setdiff(covars_qg_mam, removable_mam)
    f_qg_mam <- build_qg_mam_formula(qg_exposure_vars_mam, covars_qg_mam)
  }
  mam_qg <- if (exists("mf_qg_mam") && !is.null(mf_qg_mam) && nrow(mf_qg_mam) > 0) middle_men[as.integer(rownames(mf_qg_mam)), , drop = FALSE] else middle_men[0, , drop = FALSE]
  if (nrow(mam_qg) > 0) mam_qg$weight_normalized <- mam_qg$weight_combined / mean(mam_qg$weight_combined, na.rm = TRUE)
  fit_qg_mam <- if (nrow(mam_qg) > 0 && length(qg_exposure_vars_mam) >= 3) {
    tryCatch(
      qgcomp::qgcomp.noboot(
        f = f_qg_mam, expnms = qg_exposure_vars_mam, data = mam_qg,
        family = gaussian(), q = q_qg, weights = mam_qg$weight_normalized
      ),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )
  } else NULL
  tab_mam2 <- safe_qgcomp_extract(
    fit_qg_mam,
    "Middle-aged men: Overall mixture effect",
    nrow(mam_qg)
  ) %>%
    mutate(
      beta = fmt(beta),
      conf.low = fmt(conf.low),
      conf.high = fmt(conf.high),
      p.value = fmt_p(p.value),
      interpretation = "Change in PhenoAge acceleration (years)"
    )
  kable(tab_mam2, caption = paste0(
    "Middle-aged men (", config$middle_age_min, "-", config$middle_age_max,
    " y): descriptive overall mixture association (qgcomp)."
  ))

  # MAM Table 3 (interaction model + marginal effects)
  mam_int_obj <- fit_svy_with_covar_guard(middle_men, "phenoage_accel", c("mixture_qscore * ckd"), covars_mam, "Middle-aged men interaction")
  fit_int_mam <- mam_int_obj$fit
  n_mam_int <- mam_int_obj$n_model
  if (!is.null(fit_int_mam)) {
    int_tidy_mam <- broom::tidy(fit_int_mam, conf.int = TRUE)
    cf_names_mam <- names(coef(fit_int_mam))
    int_terms_mam <- c("mixture_qscore", cf_names_mam[str_detect(cf_names_mam, "^ckd$|mixture_qscore:ckd")])
    tab_mam3_raw <- int_tidy_mam %>%
      filter(term %in% int_terms_mam) %>%
      transmute(
        term,
        beta = fmt(estimate),
        conf.low = fmt(conf.low),
        conf.high = fmt(conf.high),
        p.value = fmt_p(p.value),
        N = n_mam_int,
        model = "Middle-aged men: Mixture*CKD interaction"
      )
    int_name_mam <- cf_names_mam[str_detect(cf_names_mam, "mixture_qscore:ckd")]
    if (length(int_name_mam) == 1) {
      c0 <- setNames(rep(0, length(cf_names_mam)), cf_names_mam); c0["mixture_qscore"] <- 1
      c1 <- setNames(rep(0, length(cf_names_mam)), cf_names_mam); c1["mixture_qscore"] <- 1; c1[int_name_mam] <- 1
      ctr0 <- svycontrast(fit_int_mam, c0)
      ctr1 <- svycontrast(fit_int_mam, c1)
      ddf <- fit_int_mam$df.residual; if (is.null(ddf) || !is.finite(ddf)) ddf <- Inf
      mk <- function(ctr, lbl) {
        est <- as.numeric(coef(ctr)); se <- as.numeric(SE(ctr)); p <- 2 * pt(-abs(est / se), df = ddf)
        tibble(term = lbl, beta = fmt(est), conf.low = fmt(est - qt(0.975, ddf) * se),
               conf.high = fmt(est + qt(0.975, ddf) * se), p.value = fmt_p(p), N = n_mam_int,
               model = "Middle-aged men: Marginal effects")
      }
      p_int <- int_tidy_mam %>% filter(term == int_name_mam) %>% pull(p.value)
      p_int <- if (length(p_int) > 0) p_int[1] else NA_real_
      tab_mam3 <- bind_rows(
        tab_mam3_raw,
        mk(ctr0, "Mixture effect among CKD=0"),
        mk(ctr1, "Mixture effect among CKD=1"),
        tibble(term = "P-interaction", beta = "", conf.low = "", conf.high = "", p.value = fmt_p(p_int), N = n_mam_int, model = "Middle-aged men: Marginal effects")
      )
    } else {
      tab_mam3 <- tab_mam3_raw
    }
  } else {
    tab_mam3 <- tibble(
      term = c("mixture_qscore", "ckd", "mixture_qscore:ckd"),
      beta = "NA", conf.low = "NA", conf.high = "NA", p.value = "NA",
      N = n_mam_int, model = "Middle-aged men: interaction (not estimable)"
    )
  }
  kable(tab_mam3, caption = paste0(
    "Middle-aged men (", config$middle_age_min, "-", config$middle_age_max,
    " y): interaction model with design-based marginal effects."
  ))

  # MAM Table 4 (sensitivities)
  fit_mam_ckd_egfr <- fit_svy_with_covar_guard(
    middle_men %>% mutate(ckd_sens = ifelse(!is.na(egfr), as.integer(egfr < 60), NA_integer_)) %>% filter(!is.na(ckd_sens)),
    "phenoage_accel", c("mixture_qscore", "ckd_sens"), mam_int_obj$realized, "MAM CKD redefined as eGFR<60"
  )
  fit_mam_no_acute <- fit_svy_with_covar_guard(
    middle_men %>% filter(is.na(crp_acute) | crp_acute == 0L),
    "phenoage_accel", c("mixture_qscore", "ckd"), mam_int_obj$realized, "MAM exclude CRP > 1 mg/dL"
  )
  tab_mam4 <- bind_rows(
    get_mix_row(fit_mam_ckd_egfr$fit, "CKD redefined as eGFR<60", fit_mam_ckd_egfr$n_model),
    get_mix_row(fit_mam_no_acute$fit, "Exclude participants with CRP > 1 mg/dL", fit_mam_no_acute$n_model)
  ) %>%
    transmute(
      sensitivity,
      beta = fmt(estimate),
      conf.low = fmt(conf.low),
      conf.high = fmt(conf.high),
      p.value = fmt_p(p.value),
      N,
      model = "Middle-aged men: Sensitivity"
    )
  kable(tab_mam4, caption = paste0(
    "Middle-aged men (", config$middle_age_min, "-", config$middle_age_max,
    " y): sensitivity analyses."
  ))
} else {
  kable(tibble(message = paste0("No middle-aged men available in primary interaction sample for age ", config$middle_age_min, "-", config$middle_age_max, ".")),
        caption = "Middle-aged men subgroup analysis status.")
}
```

# Figures

```{r figure}
dir.create("figures", showWarnings = FALSE)
n_chems <- length(primary_exposure_vars)

# Figure 1: adjusted survey-weighted predicted lines by CKD status.
pred_lines <- tryCatch({
  pdat_list <- lapply(c(0L, 1L), function(ck) {
    sub_df <- qdat %>% filter(ckd == ck)
    svy_sub <- svydesign(id = ~psu, strata = ~strata, weights = ~weight_combined, nest = TRUE, data = sub_df)
    fit_pred <- svyglm(phenoage_accel ~ mixture_qscore, design = svy_sub, family = gaussian())
    score_seq <- seq(min(sub_df$mixture_qscore, na.rm = TRUE),
                     max(sub_df$mixture_qscore, na.rm = TRUE), length.out = 100)
    newdat <- data.frame(mixture_qscore = score_seq)
    preds <- predict(fit_pred, newdata = newdat, se.fit = TRUE, type = "response")
    newdat$fit <- as.numeric(preds$fit)
    newdat$se <- as.numeric(preds$se.fit)
    newdat$ckd <- ifelse(ck == 1L, "CKD", "No CKD")
    newdat
  })
  bind_rows(pdat_list)
}, error = function(e) NULL)

# Overall marginal direction: as mixture increases, what happens to phenoage_accel (pooled, age-adjusted)
overall_direction <- tryCatch({
  svy_full <- svydesign(id = ~psu, strata = ~strata, weights = ~weight_combined, nest = TRUE, data = qdat)
  fit_overall <- svyglm(phenoage_accel ~ mixture_qscore, design = svy_full, family = gaussian())
  score_seq <- seq(min(qdat$mixture_qscore, na.rm = TRUE), max(qdat$mixture_qscore, na.rm = TRUE), length.out = 100)
  newdat <- data.frame(mixture_qscore = score_seq)
  preds <- predict(fit_overall, newdata = newdat, se.fit = TRUE, type = "response")
  newdat$fit <- as.numeric(preds$fit)
  newdat
}, error = function(e) NULL)

# Horizontal offset so green (No CKD) and orange (CKD) dots don't overlap at each quantile
x_range <- diff(range(qdat$mixture_qscore, na.rm = TRUE))
pt_offset <- max(0.005, x_range * 0.01)
qdat_plot <- qdat %>%
  mutate(
    x_plot = mixture_qscore + ifelse(ckd == 0L, -pt_offset, pt_offset),
    ckd_label = factor(ifelse(ckd == 0L, "No CKD", "CKD"), levels = c("No CKD", "CKD"))
  )

fig1 <- ggplot(qdat_plot, aes(x = x_plot, y = phenoage_accel)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = ckd_label), alpha = 0.08, size = 0.7) +
  labs(
    x = "Mixture quantile score",
    y = "PhenoAge acceleration (years)",
    color = "CKD status"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("No CKD" = "#1b9e77", "CKD" = "#d95f02")) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(alpha = 1, size = 2.5))) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  theme(legend.position = "top")

if (!is.null(pred_lines)) {
  fig1 <- fig1 +
    geom_ribbon(
      data = pred_lines,
      aes(x = mixture_qscore, ymin = fit - 1.96 * se, ymax = fit + 1.96 * se, fill = ckd),
      alpha = 0.2, inherit.aes = FALSE
    ) +
    geom_line(
      data = pred_lines,
      aes(x = mixture_qscore, y = fit, color = ckd),
      linewidth = 1.0, inherit.aes = FALSE
    ) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("No CKD" = "#1b9e77", "CKD" = "#d95f02")) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("No CKD" = "#1b9e77", "CKD" = "#d95f02"))
}
# Dotted line: overall direction of association (as mixture increases, phenoage_accel changes)
if (!is.null(overall_direction)) {
  fig1 <- fig1 +
    geom_line(
      data = overall_direction,
      aes(x = mixture_qscore, y = fit),
      linetype = "dotted", color = "gray25", linewidth = 0.9, inherit.aes = FALSE
    )
}
fig1
ggsave("figures/fig1_mixture_phenoage.jpg", plot = fig1, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300, device = "jpeg", quality = 95, bg = "white")

# Figure 2: forest plot of marginal mixture effects (design-based).
fig2 <- NULL
if (!is.null(fit_int)) {
  cf_names <- names(coef(fit_int))
  int_name <- cf_names[str_detect(cf_names, "mixture_qscore:ckd")]
  if (length(int_name) == 1 && "mixture_qscore" %in% cf_names) {
    c_ckd0 <- setNames(rep(0, length(cf_names)), cf_names); c_ckd0["mixture_qscore"] <- 1
    c_ckd1 <- setNames(rep(0, length(cf_names)), cf_names); c_ckd1["mixture_qscore"] <- 1; c_ckd1[int_name] <- 1
    ctr0 <- svycontrast(fit_int, c_ckd0)
    ctr1 <- svycontrast(fit_int, c_ckd1)
    ddf <- fit_int$df.residual; if (is.null(ddf) || !is.finite(ddf)) ddf <- Inf

    mk_row <- function(ctr, grp) {
      est <- as.numeric(coef(ctr)); se <- as.numeric(SE(ctr))
      tibble(
        group = grp,
        estimate = est,
        conf.low = est - qt(0.975, ddf) * se,
        conf.high = est + qt(0.975, ddf) * se,
        p.value = 2 * pt(-abs(est / se), df = ddf)
      )
    }
    forest_df <- bind_rows(
      mk_row(ctr0, "No CKD"),
      mk_row(ctr1, "CKD")
    ) %>%
      mutate(group = factor(group, levels = c("No CKD", "CKD")))

    fig2 <- ggplot(forest_df, aes(x = estimate, y = group, color = group)) +
      geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray40") +
      geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high), height = 0.18, linewidth = 0.8) +
      geom_point(size = 2.8) +
      geom_text(aes(label = paste0("p=", format(round(p.value, 3), nsmall = 3))),
                nudge_y = 0.2, size = 3, color = "black", show.legend = FALSE) +
      scale_color_manual(values = c("No CKD" = "#1b9e77", "CKD" = "#d95f02")) +
      labs(
        title = "Marginal mixture effects by CKD status",
        subtitle = "Design-based contrasts from interaction model",
        x = "Beta (95% CI) for mixture score",
        y = NULL
      ) +
      theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
      theme(legend.position = "none")
    fig2
    ggsave("figures/fig2_forest_marginal_effects.jpg", plot = fig2, width = 7, height = 4, dpi = 300, device = "jpeg", quality = 95, bg = "white")
  }
}
```

# Cautions and Limitations

- Cross-sectional NHANES data do not establish temporality.
- PhenoAge includes kidney-related biomarkers (creatinine, albumin), creating potential circularity with CKD definitions. Sensitivity analyses recompute PhenoAge excluding (a) creatinine only and (b) both creatinine and albumin; both are non-standard derivatives and interpreted as robustness checks.
- PhenoAge uses Levine (2018) coefficients with correct units: albumin g/L (NHANES g/dL × 10), creatinine µmol/L (mg/dL × 88.4), glucose mmol/L (mg/dL ÷ 18.018). Model B uses phenoage_accel (age-residualized) and does not adjust for age again. Mixture quantiles are computed within each NHANES cycle to avoid temporal confounding (PFAS trends 2003–2018). Survey design uses full PSU/strata when possible; if the design fails (e.g. singleton PSUs), a weights-only fallback is used and noted.
- `qgcomp` point estimates are presented descriptively (pooled sampling weights applied, but PSU/strata not incorporated for variance estimation). Statistical inference relies on the survey-weighted regression models (`svyglm`) above. Bootstrap variance estimation incorporating replicate weights was not implemented because NHANES does not provide replicate weights for environmental subsamples across all cycles.
- LOD handling follows the NHANES convention when LC indicators are available: values flagged below LOD (LC == 1) are replaced with value / sqrt(2). When no LC indicator is available, values are left unchanged (no fallback NA imputation), which improves transparency but can reduce complete-case sample size.
- **Weight selection**: a weight audit table is printed so the analyst can verify the correct weight for the specific exposure/biomarker subsample. By default WTMEC2YR is used; if the chosen exposure family requires a subsample weight (e.g., PFAS in certain cycles), the analyst should set `config$weight_type` to the appropriate column name after reviewing the audit.
- The primary PFAS mixture was defined as a fixed four-chemical set available across all retained cycles (PFOS, PFOA, PFNA, and PFHxS), with complete-case requirement on that set. PFDA was not consistently available across cycles and was therefore excluded from the primary mixture and evaluated only in supplementary analyses. Strict complete-case (qgcomp-compatible PFAS subset) modeling is reported as sensitivity. A sensitivity analysis using an available-exposure (adaptive) mixture can be added to assess robustness when using broader PFAS information.
- Exposure quantile cutpoints are defined using pooled NHANES sampling weights.
- PhenoAgeAccel is computed as the residual from a survey-weighted regression of PhenoAge on chronological age (`svyglm`), ensuring the construct is population-representative. An unweighted residualization is included as sensitivity.
- CRP unit detection uses a two-part rule: (1) table-name provenance (HSCRP tables imply mg/L), and (2) a conservative distribution heuristic (median > 1.5 **and** p75 > 3). If either triggers, CRP is converted from mg/L to mg/dL (/ 10). Cycle-level diagnostic tables are printed. PhenoAge is recomputed after any conversions. Glucose is converted from mmol/L to mg/dL if median < 6.
- Extreme biomarker values are handled by plausibility filters (WBC > 50, glucose > 600, ALP > 500 set to missing). CRP > 1 mg/dL is retained in primary analyses and evaluated in sensitivity analyses excluding potential acute inflammatory states.
- Glucose is sourced from MEC biochemistry only (`glu_mec`: BIOPRO/L40) for the primary analysis so that it aligns with MEC weights (`WTMEC2YR`). The separate GLU table (fasting) is joined with renamed variables (`LBXGLU_GLU`, `LBXSGL_GLU`) to avoid duplicate columns; it is not used in the primary model.
- Marginal effects in the interaction model (Table 3) use `svycontrast()` with design-based degrees of freedom and t-distribution p-values, maintaining full complex-survey consistency.
- Because complete-case requirements can collapse factor levels in the interaction model frame, Model B reports both intended and realized covariate sets; unsupported factor terms are dropped with explicit logging.

# Reproducibility

```{r session-info}
sessionInfo()
```
